name: Tibble v. Edison International
question: Does ERISA's six-year statute of repose bar a claim that plan fiduciaries breached their duty of prudence by offering higher-cost mutual funds to plan participants, even though identical lower-cost mutual funds were available, when fiduciaries initially chose the higher-cost mutual funds more than six years before the claim was filed?
description: A case in which the Court held that, according to trust law, the fiduciary duty includes a continuing duty to monitor investments, and that a breach of that duty within six years of the filing of a lawsuit under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act is sufficient to allow the case to proceed.
